title: SQL injection in n8n SeaTable node row:search via unescaped query interpolation
description: The SeaTable node's row:search operation builds the SQL string sent to the SeaTable SQL gateway by directly interpolating the node parameters tableName, searchColumn and searchTerm into a query template without any identifier or string escaping. When a workflow feeds external user input (for example from a form trigger or webhook) into these parameters via expressions, an attacker can break out of the intended WHERE clause and inject arbitrary SQL, retrieving rows outside the workflow's intended row-level filtering. The companion row:get operation has the same flaw via the rowId parameter.
